slides.title: American Express Earnings Conference Call
slides.speaker_notes: Cover — Amex Q1 2026 earnings conference call.

slides.title: Business Highlights

content.headline: Business Highlights
content.subtitle: Q1 2026
bullets: Q1 2026 revenue growth of 11%, and EPS of $4.28, up 18% YoY
Named the Official Payments Partner of the NFL; multi-year NBA partnership extension incl. WNBA
Launched American Express Graphite Business Cash Unlimited Card — integrated solutions expansion
Announced Amex Agentic Commerce Experiences developer kit and Amex Agent Purchase Protection
Next phase of Resy dining platform; planned Resy and Tock venue network integration
Opened Las Vegas Sidecar and New Delhi Centurion Lounge; more spaces announced
Ranked #4 on Great Place to Work 2026 list of the 100 Best Companies to Work For in the U.S.
content.so_what: Revenue, EPS, partnerships, products, lounges, and culture headlines.

panels.title: Statistical Tables reference
panels.body: Refer to the Statistical Tables for Q1 2026 on ir.americanexpress.com for defined terms.

slides.speaker_notes: Seven highlight bullets; footnoted IR terms live under disclosure.

slides.title: Total Billed Business

content.subtitle: % Increase/(decrease) vs. Prior year (FX-adjusted unless otherwise stated)
content.so_what: FX-adj billings at 9% in Q1'26; reported 10%.

slides.speaker_notes: Solid FX-adj series vs dashed Reported; G&S/T&E table under chart.

content.subtitle: % Increase/(decrease) vs. Prior year
content.so_what: UCS billings +10% in Q1'26; Gen-Z +38% YoY.

slides.title: U.S. Consumer Platinum Performance

content.subtitle: Spend Growth is Accelerating · Retention Rates Remain High and Very Stable
content.so_what: Spend accelerating post-refresh; retention high and stable (~+6pp callout).

slides.speaker_notes: Dual panel approximation of spend acceleration + retention bands.

slides.title: U.S. Consumer: Membership Model Engagement

content.subtitle: Card Member Spend Growth Rates; % Increase/(decrease) vs. Prior year
bullets: Lodging: Fine Hotels + Resorts / Hotel Collection 50% vs UCS Lodging 5% · 10x
Restaurants: U.S. Resy Restaurants 20% vs UCS Restaurants 10% · 2x
Airlines: Member Airfares 21% vs UCS Airlines 11% · 2x
content.so_what: Membership assets drive multiplies of category spend growth.

panels.title: Member Airfares footnote
panels.body: Member Airfares offers Platinum and Centurion Card Members reduced fares on select premium international and domestic tickets booked through American Express Travel.

slides.speaker_notes: Three category multipliers for membership model engagement.

slides.title: Membership Model Engagement: Proprietary Lodging Assets

content.subtitle: Lodging Spend Growth · Fine Hotels + Resorts and The Hotel Collection
bullets: Program Benefits: Early check-in, Late check-out, Room upgrades, Complimentary breakfast and Wi-Fi, Experience credits*
300+ new properties selected out of ~1,400 applications in 2026
content.so_what: Proprietary lodging network + $600 statement credit drive 10x spend growth.

slides.speaker_notes: KPI cards + lodging spend growth differential.

content.subtitle: % Increase/(decrease) vs. Prior year (FX-adjusted)
content.so_what: Commercial billings +4% with SME 81% of mix.

content.subtitle: % Increase/(decrease) vs. Prior year (FX-adjusted unless otherwise stated)
content.so_what: ICS +13% FX-adj; reported series far more volatile.

content.subtitle: % Increase/(decrease) vs. Prior year
Proprietary New Cards Acquired (in millions)
content.so_what: 3.1M proprietary new cards in Q1'26; 73% Millennial/Gen-Z of consumer NAA.

panels.title: NCA / NAA definitions
panels.body: NCA = new cards issued net of replacements. NAA = new Card Member accounts opened, excludes supplemental cards. *Fee-paying excludes Corporate.

content.subtitle: % Increase/(decrease) vs. Prior year (FX-adjusted)
Net write-off rates and 30+ days past due

content.headline: 2026 Guidance
content.subtitle: Subject to macroeconomic environment and contingencies
content.so_what: Reaffirm roadmap: high-single to low-double-digit revenue and EPS band.

panels.title: Caution
panels.body: Ability to achieve 2026 guidance is subject to macro environment and factors beyond control. See Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements.

content.headline: Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements
bullets: This presentation includes forward-looking statements within the meaning of the PSLRA
Actual results may differ materially from those expressed or implied
Factors include macroscopic, competitive, credit, regulatory, and operational risks

content.headline: Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements
bullets: Net card fee revenues may not grow consistent with 2026 expectations
Card Member engagement, attrition, and spend mix can diverge from plan
Competition may pressure fee and lend economics

content.headline: Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements
bullets: Actual Card Member rewards and services spend may differ from plan
Business development and partnership costs are uncertain
Technology and cyber incidents could disrupt operations

content.headline: Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements
bullets: Tax rate may not remain consistent with expectations
Changes in tax law, audits, and valuation allowances
Capital and liquidity requirements may constrain returns

content.headline: Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements
bullets: Commercial payments leadership and product rollout execution risk
Integration and scaling of new commercial solutions
Third-party and network partner dependencies

content.headline: Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements
bullets: Dining strategy and platform growth (Resy/Tock) execution risk
Regulatory, litigation, and reputational matters
Other risks detailed in Amex SEC filings

content.headline: American Express
content.subtitle: Q1'26 Earnings
